title: The Insider Sale That Wasn't: Arista's Pullback Is an AI Networking Gift
subtitle: Bechtolsheim took 0.25% off the table after a 10x return. The market panicked. The fundamentals didn't blink.
summary: Arista Networks dropped 13% from highs after co-founder Andy Bechtolsheim sold 0.25% of his stake. But with 35%+ revenue growth, 38% net margins, a new 1.6T AI networking platform, and unanimous analyst bullishness, the pullback looks more like an entry point than an exit signal.
bodyHtml:
$ANET dropped 6.94% in a single session on June 5. Headlines screamed about co-founder Andy Bechtolsheim dumping $73.88M worth of shares. Insider sale. Red flag. Run for the hills. Except none of that is the real story.

Bechtolsheim sold 460,000 shares across June 4-5. Scary number. But context deletes the panic: he still holds 182.8 million shares worth roughly $28.6 billion. This sale was 0.25% of his position.

The shares came from exercising options with a $14-$15 strike price. He sold at $158-$167. That's profit-taking, not a distress signal. If your cost basis was fourteen bucks and the stock was at one-sixty, you'd take a slice too.

The market didn't care about math. It cared about the headline. Arista slid 6.94% that day, extending a pullback that has now clipped 13% off the $179.80 all-time high. Welcome to the dip.

▶ Arista Networks: AI, Cloud Spending & Options Trades
Arista Networks: AI, Cloud Spending & Options Trades — The Signal

The Numbers Under The Noise

Strip the drama and what's left is one of the cleanest AI infrastructure plays on the market.

$9.71BTTM Revenue
+35.1%YoY Growth
$3.72BNet Income
38.3%Profit Margin
$4.36BFree Cash Flow
44.9%FCF Margin
$156.40Current Price
$196.9BMarket Cap
53.75xTrailing P/E
35.15xForward P/E
+19.4%YTD Return
$85.58-$179.8052-Week Range

Revenue growing better than 35% year-over-year. A 38.3% net margin that most SaaS companies would sacrifice a kidney for. Free cash flow at 44.9% of revenue.

This is not a company scraping for profitability. It's printing money while building the plumbing for the AI revolution.

The kicker: forward P/E has compressed to 35.15x. Still premium, sure. But for a company delivering these growth rates with these margins in the most important infrastructure buildout since the internet? The multiple starts looking rational fast.

The 1.6T Flex

On June 9, Arista launched its 7060XE7 Series — a 1.6 terabit Ethernet platform built for hyperscaler AI cluster interconnects. They're moving into the fabric layer that connects GPU clusters inside data centers burning billions on AI.

This isn't a peripheral play. The networking layer is becoming the bottleneck as GPU clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of nodes. InfiniBand has owned this space. Ethernet is coming for it.

Arista is leading that charge. The 7060XE7 puts them head-to-head with Broadcom and NVIDIA's own networking ambitions. This is a direct shot at the AI fabric throne.

If AI is a gold rush, Arista sells the pipes that move the water.

Wall Street Isn't Selling

All 27 analysts covering Arista rate it a Buy or Strong Buy. Zero holds. Zero sells. The median price target of $187 implies 19.6% upside from here.

MarketBeat bumped their target to $200. Seeking Alpha dropped a piece calling ANET "The Quiet Winner Of The AI Arms Race" on June 9.

The skepticism isn't on the demand side. It's supply. Can Arista get enough components to ship against hyperscaler orders that keep stacking up? That's the real question. If supply chains loosen, the revenue acceleration could be violent.

The Bottom Line

Bechtolsheim took 0.25% off the table at a 10x return on his option strikes. The market treated it like insider panic and handed you a 13% discount from highs.

The fundamentals haven't budged. Revenue growing 35%+. Margins north of 38%. New product line directly attacking the AI data center fabric. Stock still above both 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

Insider FUD created a gap between price and value. Those don't stay open forever.

— The Signal
